Container.Name: OpenClaw-Mission-Control
Container.Overview: OpenClaw Mission Control is an AI agent orchestration platform. This single-container edition bundles PostgreSQL, Redis, the backend API, the webhook worker, and the Next.js frontend — no external services required. Access the UI on the port mapped to 3000 and the API on the port mapped to 8000.

Config.Name: Frontend UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the Next.js frontend (browser UI).

Config.Name: Backend API Port
Config.Description: Port for the FastAPI backend (REST API).

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for PostgreSQL data, Redis snapshots, and application configuration.

Config.Name: AUTH_MODE
Config.Description: Authentication mode. Use 'local' for token-based auth or 'clerk' for Clerk JWT auth.

Config.Name: LOCAL_AUTH_TOKEN
Config.Description: Required when AUTH_MODE=local. Must be at least 50 characters. Use a strong random value.

Config.Name: BASE_URL
Config.Description: Public URL the backend advertises in generated gateway instructions and webhook flows. Update when behind a reverse proxy (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8000).

Config.Name: CORS_ORIGINS
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of allowed CORS origins. Set to the browser-facing URL of the frontend (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:3000).

Config.Name: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
Config.Description: URL used by the browser to reach the backend API. Use 'auto' to target port 8000 on the same host, or supply an explicit URL (e.g. http://192.168.1.10:8000).

Config.Name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
Config.Description: PostgreSQL password. PostgreSQL only listens on 127.0.0.1 inside the container, but it is still good practice to set a strong, unique value.

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Backend log level. One of: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR.
